1 / 15
A Family Proposal · Xmas & New Year 2026

Two Weeks
in Sri Lanka

Eight days on the sun-soaked south coast. Six days high in the misty mountains. Beaches, safaris, parties, and the world's most scenic train ride.

🌊 Beach
🐆 Safari
🎉 NYE Parties
🚂 Mountain Train
Leg One · 8 Nights

The South Coast

We fly in around December 23rd and head straight south to Unawatuna — a sweeping, palm-fringed bay with calm turquoise water by day and genuine beach nightlife after dark. Our base for Christmas and New Year's Eve.

Unawatuna Base
28–32°C
Peak Season
★ Must Do

Galle Fort

A 17th-century UNESCO Dutch fort just 20 minutes from our base. Thick stone ramparts overlooking the Indian Ocean, narrow cobbled streets, rooftop bars, and boutique cafés tucked into colonial buildings. Perfect for an afternoon wandering into an evening of cocktails at sunset.

📍 20 mins from Unawatuna Half-day trip — easily combined with a beach afternoon. Best visited at golden hour when the ramparts glow.
★ Must Do

Yala National Park

Home to the world's highest density of leopards. A full-day jeep safari roughly 2 hours from Unawatuna — leopards, wild elephants, crocodiles, and over 200 bird species in a dramatic landscape of scrub jungle and lagoons.

📍 ~2 hrs from Unawatuna · Full Day Early morning start for best wildlife sightings. One of the unmissable wildlife experiences in Asia.
★ Must Do

Sigiriya Rock Fortress

A 5th-century royal palace perched atop a 200-metre granite monolith rising from the jungle. UNESCO World Heritage Site. Climb through the ancient lion's gate to ruins at the summit with jaw-dropping 360° views. Combine with the Dambulla Cave Temples on the same day.

📍 ~3.5 hrs north · Long Day Trip Best done by private vehicle. An early start means cooler temperatures for the climb.
December 31st
New Year's Eve
on the Beach

Just 30 minutes from Unawatuna, Hikkaduwa hosts Sri Lanka's biggest and most energetic New Year's Eve beach party.

🎆
Hikkaduwa NYEBonfires, DJs, beachfront bars, fire shows, and fireworks over the Indian Ocean at midnight.
🌍
International CrowdDecember is peak season. Bars are packed with Australians, Europeans and fellow travellers — a real scene, not a quiet resort.
🏖️
Options for EveryoneStay local in Unawatuna for a relaxed evening, or make the short trip to Hikkaduwa for the full party experience.
Leg Two · 6 Nights

Into the Hills

After the coast, we head north and up into Sri Lanka's extraordinary hill country. Cool, misty mornings. Emerald tea plantations to the horizon. The world's most scenic train taking us there.

Kandy
Ella
Nuwara Eliya
15–25°C
★ Must Do

Kandy–Ella Scenic Train

Widely rated the most scenic train journey in the world. Six hours winding through cloud forests, rolling tea estates, and dramatic mountain viaducts. The views are simply extraordinary — this is as much an attraction as any destination on the trip.

🚂 6 hours · Kandy to Ella Book seats in advance. Window seats on the right side heading south for the best views.
★ Must Do

Nine Arch Bridge

Sri Lanka's most iconic image — a nine-span colonial-era stone viaduct built in 1921 without a single piece of steel, rising from a lush jungle gorge near Ella. The sight of the train crossing through the tree canopy is one of those genuinely magical travel moments.

📍 Short walk from Ella town Time your visit to coincide with a scheduled train crossing. Check the timetable at your hotel.
Also Great

Horton Plains & World's End

Trek through a misty high-altitude plateau to World's End — a sheer cliff dropping dramatically into the valley far below. The walk passes through cloud forest, past Baker's Falls waterfall, and through landscapes that feel truly otherworldly.

📍 Near Nuwara Eliya · Half Day Trek Arrive before 9am — clouds roll in by mid-morning and obscure the famous view. Bring a light jacket; it's cool up here.
Also Great

Tea Plantation Tour

The rolling emerald estates around Nuwara Eliya produce some of the finest Ceylon tea in the world. Tour a working factory, walk the rows alongside the pickers, and finish with a tasting session — the best cup of tea you'll ever drink, in the very valley where it grew.

📍 Nuwara Eliya · 2–3 Hours Pedro Tea Estate and Mackwoods Labookellie are both highly rated and easy to visit independently.
Also Great

Little Adam's Peak

A manageable 30-minute climb through a working tea estate to a 1,141m summit with 360° panoramic views across the Ella valley and the mountain ranges beyond. Accessible for all fitness levels and utterly worth the effort.

📍 Ella · Half Day Go early morning or late afternoon for the best light. The path winds through the Greenland Tea Estate.
A Message for James
James…
You know you want to.

Still not convinced? Here's what people are actually saying about the nightlife where we're staying.

"

Unawatuna Beach is famous for its nightlife. The beach is lined with bars and clubs that offer great music and drinks... a completely different side of Sri Lanka.

— Travejar Travel Guide
"

Lucky Tuna, Unawatuna: reggae by day, EDM at night, monthly Full Moon Parties. Hikkaduwa hosts beach raves and live DJ nights during peak season (Dec–Apr), drawing a young and energetic crowd.

— Hopscotch Travels Nightlife Guide 2026
"

Hikkaduwa is the place to party in Sri Lanka. The main strip is peppered with bars and clubs. The town has become a favourite escape for many European visitors when winter descends on their continent.

— Eats & Retreats · Timeout Sri Lanka
"

Hikkaduwa is known for nightlife and large gatherings. It's one of the loudest and most energetic New Year party places in Sri Lanka. Young crowds come here for dancing, bonfires, and fireworks.

— Wanderon: New Year in Sri Lanka
"

All three places are crowded with young drinkers on 31st night… a good crowd of international tourists.

— TripAdvisor Forum: NYE in Sri Lanka
Beach bars. Bonfires. International crowd.
All of it, Christmas Day included.

We're not talking a quiet family resort. We're talking peak-season south coast Sri Lanka — one of Asia's best-kept party secrets, with some of the world's greatest wildlife and scenery thrown in for free.

The Verdict

So —
are we going?

Christmas on a palm-fringed beach. A leopard safari. New Year's Eve by the Indian Ocean. Then the world's most beautiful train ride into misty mountains with hikes that'll leave you speechless.

The nightlife is real. The crowd is international. The landscapes are extraordinary — all for a fraction of the Maldives price.

✈️ ~10hrs from Australia
🌊 Perfect Dec Weather
🎉 Real NYE Parties
🐆 Leopard Safari
🚂 World's Best Train
💰 Far Cheaper than Maldives
